denial_letter_text:
NOTICE OF ADVERSE BENEFIT DETERMINATION

Date of Notice: 03/04/2026
UnitedHealthcare Choice Plus (employer-sponsored group health plan)
Member ID: W100316760          Group #: GRP-1040
Authorization / Reference #: PA-2026-109501
Claim #: CLM-2026-190121
Date of Service (requested): 02/12/2026
Treating provider (as submitted): Northline Medical Group

Dear Member,

This document is notice of an adverse benefit determination regarding prior authorization. We received your provider's prior authorization request on 02/14/2026 for Subcutaneous mastectomy related to Gynecomastia, persistent adolescent (N62). After clinical review, we are unable to approve this request.

CASE SUMMARY
Service requested: Subcutaneous mastectomy
Diagnosis (as submitted): Gynecomastia, persistent adolescent (N62)
Denial code: CO-197 — Precertification/authorization absent — service requires prior approval before the date of service.
Clinical policy applied: CDG-UM100 under UnitedHealthcare Coverage Determination Guideline (CDG) and applicable Medical Policy Bulletin

EXPLANATION OF DECISION
Our review applied the criteria in CDG-UM100 and your plan's Summary Plan Description (SPD), Section 4 — Medical Benefits and Utilization Management. The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. Emergency imaging/treatment for Gynecomastia, persistent adolescent (N62) is denied for lack of prior authorization. Retroactive authorization is not granted for non-emergent services.

Specifically, the information provided does not satisfy the utilization-management requirements associated with emergency retroactive auth. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. Diagnosis and procedure codes used in this determination are available upon request, as required by applicable federal standards.

If your provider wishes to discuss this determination, they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the verbal timeframe communicated to office staff (not confirmed in this letter).

Your treating physician may request a peer-to-peer discussion with a UnitedHealthcare medical director. Peer-to-peer review must be requested before a formal appeal is initiated for the same authorization decision.

YOUR RIGHT TO INFORMATION
You may request a copy of your claim file, including documents, clinical criteria, and other information we considered in making this determination. Submit your request to the address shown on this notice or through the member portal identified in your plan materials.

APPEAL RIGHTS
You have the right to appeal this determination. If this denial is upheld after internal review, you may have the right to request an independent external review of our decision at no cost to you, subject to applicable federal and state requirements. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this notice. Include your member identification number (W100316760) and reference PA-2026-109501. You may also have the right to receive, upon request and without charge, copies of documents relevant to this determination.

Please note that benefits are subject to all terms, exclusions, and limitations of your group health plan document. This letter constitutes the formal adverse determination for the service identified above.

Sincerely,

UnitedHealthcare Utilization Management
Clinical Operations

This determination was made by Dr. J. Smith, Medical Director.
clinical_context: The 64-year-old member has Gynecomastia, persistent adolescent (N62). She has been treated under commercial coverage with Subcutaneous mastectomy recommended as the next appropriate step. Tanner V, persistence 24 months, psychosocial impairment documented. Submitted documentation supports medical necessity for Subcutaneous mastectomy in the setting of Gynecomastia, persistent adolescent (N62); the denial letter does not discuss those records or cite the objective findings in the file.
